steps.title: Identify the time signal
steps.content: The phrase "since it began in 2012" marks an action that started in the past and continues to the present moment. Standard English uses the present perfect (have/has + past participle) for this time frame.

steps.title: Match tense to signal
steps.content: The subject is plural (volunteer map editors who), so the correct form is have contributed. This links the 2012 start point to the present.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'contribute': simple present cannot pair with the "since 2012" duration phrase. ✗
  • Choice B — 'will contribute': simple future contradicts an action that has already been happening since 2012. ✗
  • Choice C — 'have contributed': present perfect correctly spans a past start to the present. ✓
  • Choice D — 'will be contributing': future progressive again ignores the past start point required by "since 2012." ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
